api/BOOSEapp.AppCanvas.html.summary: Class AppCanvas Namespace BOOSEapp Assembly BOOSEapp.dll Custom canvas implementation for the BOOSE application that handles drawing operations on bitmaps. public class AppCanvas : ICanvas Inheritance object AppCanvas Implements ICanvas Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors AppCanvas(int, int) Initializes a new instance of the AppCanvas class with the specified dimensions. public AppCanvas(int xsize, int ysize) Parameters xsize int ysize int Properties PenColour Gets or sets the current pen color used for drawing on the canvas. public object PenColour { get; set; } Property Value object Xpos Gets or sets the current X position of the drawing cursor on the canvas. public int Xpos { get; set; } Property Value int Ypos Gets or sets the current Y position of the drawing cursor on the canvas. public int Ypos { get; set; } Property Value int Methods Circle(int, bool) Draws or fills a circle at the current position with the specified radius, with an option of overriding the restrictions. public void Circle(int radius, bool filled) Parameters radius int The radius of the circle filled bool Indicates whether the circle should be filled Clear() Clears the canvas by filling it with a white background. public void Clear() DrawTo(int, int) Draws a line from the current position to the specified (x, y) coordinates. public void DrawTo(int x, int y) Parameters x int The target X coordinate. y int The target Y coordinaet. MoveTo(int, int) Moves the drawing cursor to the specified (x, y) coordinates without drawing. public void MoveTo(int x, int y) Parameters x int The target X coordinate y int The target Y coordinate Rect(int, int, bool) Draws a rectangle at the current position with the specified width and height. public void Rect(int width, int height, bool filled) Parameters width int The width of the rectangle. height int The height of the rectangle. filled bool Indicates whether the rectangle should be filled. Reset() Resets the canvas by clearing it and centering the drawing cursor. public void Reset() Set(int, int) Resizes the canvas to the specified width and height, and resets the drawing state. public void Set(int width, int height) Parameters width int height int SetColour(int, int, int) Sets the pen color using the specified RGB values. public void SetColour(int red, int green, int blue) Parameters red int green int blue int Tri(int, int) public void Tri(int width, int height) Parameters width int height int Exceptions NotImplementedException WriteText(string) Writes the specified text at the current position on the canvas. public void WriteText(string text) Parameters text string The text to draw on the canvas. getBitmap() Gets the underlying bitmap representing the canvas. public object getBitmap() Returns object

api/BOOSEapp.AppElse.html.title: Class AppElse | BOOSEapp Documentation
api/BOOSEapp.AppElse.html.summary: Class AppElse Namespace BOOSEapp Assembly BOOSEapp.dll Represents the "else" branch of a conditional command within a program sequence. public class AppElse : CompoundCommand, ICommand Inheritance object Command Evaluation Boolean ConditionalCommand CompoundCommand AppElse Implements ICommand Inherited Members CompoundCommand.ReduceRestrictions() CompoundCommand.CheckParameters(string[]) CompoundCommand.CorrespondingCommand ConditionalCommand.endLineNumber ConditionalCommand.EndLineNumber ConditionalCommand.Condition ConditionalCommand.LineNumber ConditionalCommand.CondType ConditionalCommand.ReturnLineNumber Boolean.BoolValue Evaluation.expression Evaluation.evaluatedExpression Evaluation.varName Evaluation.value Evaluation.ProcessExpression(string) Evaluation.Expression Evaluation.VarName Evaluation.Value Evaluation.Local Command.IsDouble Command.program Command.parameterList Command.parameters Command.paramsint Command.Set(StoredProgram, string) Command.ProcessParameters(string) Command.ToString() Command.Program Command.Name Command.ParameterList Command.Parameters Command.Paramsint object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks AppElse is used in conjunction with an "if" command to define alternative execution logic when the "if" condition is not met. It must directly follow a corresponding "if" command; otherwise, a BOOSE.CommandException is thrown during compilation. This class is typically used internally by the command processing framework and is not intended to be instantiated directly by user code. Constructors AppElse() Initialises a new instance of the AppElse class, and sets the conditional types to CommIF. public AppElse() Methods Compile() Compiles the "else" command by locating its matching "if" command on the program stack, linking them together, and registering the "else" command in the program sequence. If no matching "if" command is found, or if the found command is not an "if", a BOOSE.CommandException is thrown indicating a syntax error in the program. public override void Compile() Exceptions CommandException Execute() Executes the "else" command by checking the condition of its corresponding "if" command. If the "if" condition is false, the program counter is set to the line number immediately following the "else" command, allowing execution to continue with the commands within the "else" block. If the "if" condition is true, execution continues sequentially, effectively skipping the "else" block. public override void Execute()

api/BOOSEapp.AppEnd.html.title: Class AppEnd | BOOSEapp Documentation
api/BOOSEapp.AppEnd.html.summary: Class AppEnd Namespace BOOSEapp Assembly BOOSEapp.dll Represents the "end" command that marks the end of a compound command block, such as "if", "while", or "for". public class AppEnd : CompoundCommand, ICommand Inheritance object Command Evaluation Boolean ConditionalCommand CompoundCommand AppEnd Implements ICommand Inherited Members CompoundCommand.ReduceRestrictions() CompoundCommand.CheckParameters(string[]) CompoundCommand.CorrespondingCommand ConditionalCommand.endLineNumber ConditionalCommand.EndLineNumber ConditionalCommand.Condition ConditionalCommand.LineNumber ConditionalCommand.CondType ConditionalCommand.ReturnLineNumber Boolean.BoolValue Evaluation.expression Evaluation.evaluatedExpression Evaluation.varName Evaluation.value Evaluation.ProcessExpression(string) Evaluation.Expression Evaluation.VarName Evaluation.Value Evaluation.Local Command.IsDouble Command.program Command.parameterList Command.parameters Command.paramsint Command.Set(StoredProgram, string) Command.ProcessParameters(string) Command.ToString() Command.Program Command.Name Command.ParameterList Command.Parameters Command.Paramsint object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors AppEnd() Initializes a new instance of the AppEnd class public AppEnd() Methods Compile() Compiles the "end" command by popping its matching start command from teh program stack, linking teh end line number back to the start public override void Compile() Exceptions CommandException Execute() Executes the "end" command. The "for", "while", and "if" loop types, jump the program counter back to the start of the loop command if the condition is still active. public override void Execute()

api/BOOSEapp.AppFor.html.title: Class AppFor | BOOSEapp Documentation
api/BOOSEapp.AppFor.html.summary: Class AppFor Namespace BOOSEapp Assembly BOOSEapp.dll Represents a "for" loop command that iterates a variable form a starting value to an end value with an optional step icrement. The command supports syntax such as "for i = 1 to 10 step 2", where "i" is the loop variable, "1" is the starting value, "10" is the ending value, and "2" is the step. public class AppFor : ConditionalCommand, ICommand Inheritance object Command Evaluation Boolean ConditionalCommand AppFor Implements ICommand Inherited Members ConditionalCommand.endLineNumber ConditionalCommand.EndLineNumber ConditionalCommand.Condition ConditionalCommand.LineNumber ConditionalCommand.CondType ConditionalCommand.ReturnLineNumber Boolean.BoolValue Evaluation.expression Evaluation.evaluatedExpression Evaluation.varName Evaluation.value Evaluation.CheckParameters(string[]) Evaluation.ProcessExpression(string) Evaluation.Expression Evaluation.VarName Evaluation.Value Evaluation.Local Command.IsDouble Command.program Command.parameterList Command.parameters Command.paramsint Command.Set(StoredProgram, string) Command.ProcessParameters(string) Command.ToString() Command.Program Command.Name Command.ParameterList Command.Parameters Command.Paramsint object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors AppFor() Initializes a new instance of the AppFor class and sets the conditional type to "commFor". public AppFor() Properties From The starting value expression of the loop public int From { get; } Property Value int LoopVarName Gets the name of the loop control variable public string LoopVarName { get; } Property Value string Step The increment applied to the loop variable each iteration. Default is 1. public int Step { get; } Property Value int To The upper bound expression of the loop public int To { get; } Property Value int Methods Compile() Parses the "for" loop parameters, declares the loop variable if it doesn't already exist, and pushes this command onto the program stack for later linking a matching "end" command. public override void Compile() Exceptions CommandException Execute() Executes the "for" loop command. On the first execution, initializes the loop variable and evaluates the bounds and step. After the "end" command jumps back it checks whether the loop condition is satisfied public override void Execute() Exceptions StoredProgramException

api/BOOSEapp.AppPeek.html.title: Class AppPeek | BOOSEapp Documentation
api/BOOSEapp.AppPeek.html.summary: Class AppPeek Namespace BOOSEapp Assembly BOOSEapp.dll Represents the "peek" command that retrieves a value from an array at a specified index and assigns it to a variable. public class AppPeek : Command, ICommand Inheritance object Command AppPeek Implements ICommand Inherited Members Command.IsDouble Command.program Command.parameterList Command.parameters Command.paramsint Command.Set(StoredProgram, string) Command.ProcessParameters(string) Command.ToString() Command.Program Command.Name Command.ParameterList Command.Parameters Command.Paramsint object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors AppPeek() Initializes a new instance of the AppPeek class. public AppPeek() Methods CheckParameters(string[]) public override void CheckParameters(string[] parameterList) Parameters parameterList string[] Compile() Parses the peek command parameters, extracting the target varaible name, array name, and index expression. The expected syntax is "peek var = arrayName index", where "var" is the target variable to assign the value to, "arrayName", is the name of the array to peek from, and "index" is the expression for the index to peek at. public override void Compile() Exceptions CommandException Execute() Executes the peek command by evaluating the index expression, reading the value from the specified array at that index, and storing the result in the target variable. public override void Execute() Exceptions CommandException

Otherwise, the base method is called to handle the update, which may throw an exception if the variable does not exist or is of an incompatible type. public override void UpdateVariable(string varName, int value) Parameters varName string The name of the variable to update value int The integer value to assign

api/BOOSEapp.IAppCanvas.html.title: Interface IAppCanvas | BOOSEapp Documentation
api/BOOSEapp.IAppCanvas.html.summary: Interface IAppCanvas Namespace BOOSEapp Assembly BOOSEapp.dll Provides additional drawing methods for an application canvas, extending BOOSE.ICanvas. public interface IAppCanvas : ICanvas Inherited Members ICanvas.Set(int, int) ICanvas.SetColour(int, int, int) ICanvas.MoveTo(int, int) ICanvas.DrawTo(int, int) ICanvas.Clear() ICanvas.Reset() ICanvas.Circle(int, bool) ICanvas.Rect(int, int, bool) ICanvas.Tri(int, int) ICanvas.WriteText(string) ICanvas.getBitmap() ICanvas.Xpos ICanvas.Ypos ICanvas.PenColour Methods Circle(int, bool, bool) Draws a circle with the specified radius. void Circle(int radius, bool filled, bool overrideRestrictions) Parameters radius int The radius of the circle. filled bool Whether the circle is filled. overrideRestrictions bool Whether to override drawing restrictions. Rect(int, int, bool, bool) Draws a rectangle with the specified width and height. void Rect(int width, int height, bool filled, bool overrideRestrictions) Parameters width int The width of the rectangle. height int The height of the rectangle. filled bool Whether the rectangle is filled. overrideRestrictions bool Whether to override drawing restrictions.

api/BOOSEapp.html.title: Namespace BOOSEapp | BOOSEapp Documentation
api/BOOSEapp.html.summary: Namespace BOOSEapp Classes AppArray Declares an int or real array stored inside AppStoredProgram. Syntax: array int nums 10 array real prices 10 AppCall AppCanvas Custom canvas implementation for the BOOSE application that handles drawing operations on bitmaps. AppCircle Represents a command that draws a circle on the canvas with a specified radius. AppCommandFactory Factory class for custom application commands including Circle, Rect, and MoveTo. AppElse Represents the "else" branch of a conditional command within a program sequence. AppEnd Represents the "end" command that marks the end of a compound command block, such as "if", "while", or "for". AppFor Represents a "for" loop command that iterates a variable form a starting value to an end value with an optional step icrement. The command supports syntax such as "for i = 1 to 10 step 2", where "i" is the loop variable, "1" is the starting value, "10" is the ending value, and "2" is the step. AppIf AppInt Unrestricted integer variable implementation Replaces the sealed BOOSE.Int class AppMoveTo Represents a command that moves the application canvas to the specified coordinates. AppParser Extends the base Parser to support expressions and the BOOSE language features used in the BOOSE app, such as method definitions and unrestricted variable assignment. AppPeek Represents the "peek" command that retrieves a value from an array at a specified index and assigns it to a variable. AppPoke Represents the "poke" command that assigns a value to an element of an array variable. AppReal Represnts an unrestricted real variable declaration and assignment command. Replaces the sealed BOOSE.Real class. AppRect Represents a command that draws a rectangle on the canvas with specified width and height. AppStoredProgram Extends the base StoredProgram class to add support for integer and real arrays, as well as tracking limits on boolean and compound commands. AppWhile Represents the "while" compound command, which executes a block of commands repeatedly as long as a specified condition is true. AppWriteText Represents the "write" commmand that renders text string on the canvas at the current cursor position. The command can take a string literal, a variable name, or an expression that evaluates to a string as its parameter. Form1 The main form for the BOOSE application, providing UI and program execution logic. Interfaces IAppCanvas Provides additional drawing methods for an application canvas, extending BOOSE.ICanvas.
